query: mesiodistal width to height ratio maxillary canine studies

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Vertical Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The maxillary-mandibular plane angle (MMPA) assesses the vertical relationship between the maxilla and mandible. The MMPA is formed by projecting lines from the mandibular and maxillary planes until they meet posteriorly. The average MMPA value is 27 ± 4 degrees. A normal MMPA indicates a well-proportioned lower face and a normal overbite. An increased MMPA value suggests a long lower face and an open bite, while a decreased MMPA value indicates a shorter lower face and a closed bite. [15] Incisor Position: Angular Evaluation

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Incisor Position: Linear Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The linear measurement of the maxilla is taken from the maxillary incisor to nasion-A, while the linear measurement of the mandible is taken from the mandibular incisor to nasion-B. These measurements describe the relationship between the tooth and its supporting basal bone, indicating whether the tooth is in a normal, procumbent (ahead of the supporting bone), or recumbent (behind the supporting bone) position. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Anteroposterior Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: An increased SNA angle indicates that the maxilla is positioned protrusively relative to the cranial base compared to the average. [3] Conversely, a decreased SNA angle suggests that the maxilla is retruded relative to the cranial base compared to the norm. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Likewise, the smooth curve in the maxilla encompasses the central fossae of posterior teeth and the cingulum of the canines and incisors. Other ideal qualities include alignment of the maxillary and mandibular midlines with each other and the face. Angle class I is seen in around 60% of the affected population and is the most commonly seen molar relationship.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- History and Physical -- Dental anterior crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: In dental anterior crossbite, 1 or more teeth are involved. The profile is straight in centric occlusion and centric relation. Class I molar and canine relation can be seen. SNA, SNB, and ANB angles are within normal limits. It can be due to abnormal axial dental inclination.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Preparation -- Selection of Crown
retrieved_docs.content: Loss of mesiodistal dimension resulting from space loss distal to the mandibular primary first molar complicates selecting an appropriate size crown (see Image. Stainless Steel Crown in Primary First Molar). In such cases, measuring the dimension of the contralateral tooth is advised. [12] A correctly fitting crown should snap into place at try-in. [13] Other factors should be considered when selecting the crown: occlusal anatomy, primate spaces, and crown height. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Cone Beam Computed Tomography -- Clinical Significance -- Maxillary Sinus Floor Elevation
retrieved_docs.content: In the posterior maxilla, tooth replacement with dental implants requires sinus lifting surgery when the maxillary sinus is pneumatized, extending toward the alveolar process. This surgery increases bone quality and quantity in the maxilla's posterior region.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Cone Beam Computed Tomography -- Clinical Significance -- Maxillary Sinus Floor Elevation
retrieved_docs.content: If the amount of bone between the ridge crest and the maxillary sinus floor is inadequate, <5 mm, an open sinus lift procedure is indicated. Preoperative CBCT or CT before open sinus lift surgery is recommended to evaluate certain factors like membrane thickness, presence of sinus septa, alveolar antral artery trajectory, and residual bone height. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontitis -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Factors like the position of teeth in the arch also affect the prognosis of teeth having periodontitis, like the risk of mandibular canine is least while the maxillary second molar is the highest. [38] Prognosis is poor for teeth with increased periodontal probing pocket depth, mobility, bone loss, furcation involvement, malpositioned teeth, and an inadequate crown-root ratio. [39] With the improving effectiveness of oral hygiene measure practiced by the patient and discontinuation of smoking, the prognosis of the case improves along with decreasing mobility of the teeth. [39] Teeth with bleeding sites during the maintenance phase have a risk of attachment loss three times more as compared to non-bleeding sites. [40]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Macrodontia often presents an aesthetic concern in the anterior maxilla, while macrodonts in the posterior region may contribute to crowding and impede normal tooth eruption. Radiographic monitoring is essential to assess potential eruption abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Vertical Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The angular measurement of the maxilla is determined by measuring the angle from the incisor to nasion-A, while the angular measurement of the mandible is calculated from the incisor to nasion-B. These values indicate the inclination of teeth—proclined teeth are angled forward, retroclined teeth are tilted backward, and normally inclined teeth are in a neutral position. [8] The normal incisor to nasion-A angle is 22 degrees, and the normal incisor to nasion-B angle is 25 degrees. An increased incisor to nasion-A or nasion-B angle indicates proclination of the incisor, while a decreased angle suggests retroclination. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Incisor Position: Linear Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The average value for both incisor to nasion-A and incisor to nasion-B is 4 mm. An increased incisor to nasion-A or -B value indicates that the incisor is procumbent, whereas a decreased value suggests that the incisor is recumbent. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- History and Physical -- Skeletal anterior crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: Skeletal anterior crossbite is characterized by molar and canine Class III relation in centric occlusion and centric relation. An edge-to-edge incisor relation cannot be obtained in a centric relation. The malocclusion's etiology and the affected teeth' inclination should be evaluated. The upper arch expansion is more likely stable if the teeth to be moved are initially tilted palatally. The appliances used for expansion are a Coffin spring, Quad helix appliance, surgically assisted rapid maxillary expansion, and Ni-Ti palatal expander. The patient has a concave profile, a retrusive upper lip, a predominant chin, and a negative ANB angle.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Cone Beam Computed Tomography -- Clinical Significance -- Orthodontics
retrieved_docs.content: Small FOV CBCT may be indicated in the following cases as part of orthodontic treatment planning when conventional radiographs do not provide enough information: [8] Unerupted permanent maxillary canines. Dilacerated teeth. Unerupted or supernumerary teeth that are close to the inferior alveolar canal. Clefts that may require grafting. Large FOV CBCT is sometimes indicated in orthognathic surgery planning where 3D data are required. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Preparation -- Occlusal Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Before placing the rubber dam and preparing the tooth, observe the following: Whether the opposing tooth has extruded as a consequence of long-standing carious lesions Whether there is mesial drift as a result of carious lesions leading to proximal space loss Presence of spacing and crowding Examine occlusion directly in the mouth or indirectly with dental analysis casts for incisor, canine, and molar relationship on both sides Examine for dental midline and the cusp fossa relationship bilaterally

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Cephalometric Analysis -- Clinical Significance -- Anteroposterior Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: ANB angle: The ANB angle evaluates the anteroposterior relationship between the maxilla and mandible. [12] The ANB angle is calculated as the difference between the SNA (sella-nasion to A-point) and SNB (sella-nasion to B-point) angles, using the formula: ANB = SNA - SNB. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 277
retrieved_docs.content: Procedures on Nose are associated with nasal disorder (اِضطِراب أنفي).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1929
retrieved_docs.content: Nasal endoscopy with maxillary antrostomy is classified as a diagnostic procedure involving the nose, mouth, and pharynx, providing clinicians with direct endoscopic visualization and access to the maxillary sinus for assessment of sinonasal pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 243
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of teeth and jaw are classified under the broader parent category zuby and are specifically associated with the anatomical location of zuby mléčné, the primary (milk) teeth.

retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasonography of head and neck is classified as a form of diagnostic ultrasound of the head and neck.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 362
retrieved_docs.content: Other bone disease and musculoskeletal deformities are associated with bone resorption, indicating that these conditions often involve increased breakdown of bone tissue, which contributes to skeletal weakening and structural abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 0 -- Community 19
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a dental procedure that directly targets teeth (zuby) and is recognized as part of the broader set of dental procedures. Dental procedures themselves are also performed directly on deciduous teeth (zuby mléčné). Disorders of teeth and jaw are categorized under the parent concept of teeth (zuby) and are specifically located in the deciduous dentition (zuby mléčné). Consequently, both the extraction of a single tooth and other dental procedures may be applied to permanent and baby teeth, while disorders of the teeth and jaw primarily affect the baby teeth.
